title: cytisus albus subsp. pallidus
intro: Cytisus albus subsp. pallidus, also known as white broom or pale broom, is a plant species within the legume family, Fabaceae, and is a variant of the white broom (Cytisus albus). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic properties. The health benefits of Cytisus albus subsp. pallidus include its ability to alleviate symptoms of coughs, sore throats, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to help lower blood pressure and improve overall cardiovascular health. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, although its effectiveness and safety may vary depending on individual circumstances and usage.
